source_pr.title: ddl: add some debug log for unstable test TestTruncatePartitionAndDropTable
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Add debug log for https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/27889

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

From the tidb log information, the only reason I can come up with is, truncate table do not finish in time.

```
	tk.MustExec("truncate table t3;")
	partitionPrefix := tablecodec.EncodeTablePrefix(oldPID)
	hasOldPartitionData := checkPartitionDelRangeDone(c, s.testIntegrationSuite, partitionPrefix)
	c.Assert(hasOldPartitionData, IsFalse)
```

`checkPartitionDelRangeDone` will try 150 times, and sleep 100ms for each retry.
That means after "truncate table t3", the real data deletion do not finish within 15s....

I can't reproduce on my machine, and I tried several times in CI, but it doesn't reproduce either.
Maybe we can add the debug logs, so the next time it reproduce, we can catch it.

What's Changed:

How it Works:

Some background:  after "truncate table", the old table range is recorded.
And a background delete range worker will delete the range asynchronously.

In unistore, that delete range worker is emulated, it's done by delete the rows, not physically range delection.
When the load is heavy, it might be slow, cause this unit test fail.
